laws.title: New York Paid Prenatal Leave
laws.summary: Effective January 1, 2025, New York requires all private-sector employers to provide employees with 20 hours of paid prenatal leave per 52-week period. This leave covers prenatal care appointments including physical exams, medical procedures, and fertility treatments. There is no employer size threshold — all NY private-sector employers must comply.
compliance_actions: Update your employee handbook to include a paid prenatal leave policy stating the 20-hour annual entitlement
Train managers that employees cannot be required to exhaust other paid leave before using prenatal leave
Do not ask employees for a medical reason or documentation when they request prenatal leave
Pay prenatal leave at the employee's regular rate of pay (or the applicable minimum wage, whichever is higher)
laws.source: New York State Department of Labor

laws.title: New York Enacts Opioid Antagonist Requirements for Employers
laws.summary:
New York Enacts Opioid Antagonist Requirements for Employers 
 New York recently enacted legislation that will soon require employers that are federally mandated to maintain workplace first aid supplies to include an opioid antagonist, such as Naloxone or Narcan.
